summary: CUNY's ASAP three-year graduation rate fell from 44.0% in Fiscal 2022 to 36.2% in Fiscal 2024 before recovering somewhat to 38.5% in Fiscal 2025. The Fiscal 2024 low matches ASAP's own documented decline for its fall 2020 entering cohort, which graduated at a three-year rate of 36.2% compared to 39.3% for the fall 2019 cohort — a drop tied to pandemic-era enrollment disruption. ASAP remains one of CUNY's most effective programs, roughly doubling graduation rates versus similar students outside it, but the City's proposed $41 million in permanent annual cuts for Fiscal 2024–2026 threatens the program's further expansion.

sources.label: CUNY ASAP & ACE Fast Facts, April 2024

oneLiner: decreasing overall since FY22, though FY25 shows a partial recovery tied to cohort mix
